cand_a.note: Backup diagnostic paper; not needed as main candidate is strong

cand_h.note: TopK dose-response incorporated as Experiment 3 in main candidate

reasons: TopK achieves 85.5% absorption reduction (0.2258 -> 0.0328), consistent with prior full-experiment 78.0% (d=5.51)
Absorption metric discriminates structure from randomness: random 0.452 >> baseline 0.226 >> topk 0.033
Both TopK and MultiScale achieve L0=50 with similar low absorption, supporting sparsity mediation hypothesis
MCC correctly identified as degenerate (~0.21-0.22 across all variants including Random), confirming prior decision to drop it
Dead latent crisis confirmed (TopK 83.2%, MultiScale 58.4%) - genuine pathology to report transparently
Prior full experiments (iter_005, 3 variants x 5 replicates) already demonstrated robust effect sizes
Clear path to publishable result: 5-seed full experiment + L0-matched controls + k-sweep + real-LLM validation
next_actions: Run full experiment with 5 seeds on SynthSAEBench-16k: Baseline, TopK, MultiScale, L0-matched controls
Report dead_latents_pct prominently in all results
Consider alternative downstream metrics beyond MCC
Run real-LLM validation on Gemma Scope SAEs (Phase 2)
risks: Single-seed pilot lacks statistical power - mitigated by planned 5-seed full experiment
MCC variation sanity check FAIL - metric is insensitive, but absorption rate is validated and discriminating
High dead latent rates in TopK/MultiScale may artifactually suppress absorption - mitigated by reporting dead_latents_pct and considering active-latent-only absorption
